perturbation.original_text: The Restricted Party agrees not to dispute, contest, or deny any ownership rights of the Alliqua Entities of the Confidential Information or the Company Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: The Restricted Party agrees not to dispute, contest, or deny any ownership rights of the Alliqua Entities of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'or the Company Intellectual Property' from Section 4 creates an in-text contradiction. The rest of the document, particularly Section 3(c)(v), refers to the Alliqua Entities' acquisition of the Company Intellectual Property. By removing the agreement to not dispute the ownership, but also not removing the fact that it was previously mentioned, makes it contradictory as there is no confirmation or assurance that it will be respected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The Restricted Party hereby acknowledges the broad territorial scope of the covenants contained in this Agreement, but acknowledges and agrees that the restrictions are reasonable and enforceable in view of, among other things, (i) the narrow range of activities prohibited, (ii) the Company’s products and services are marketed on a wide geographic scope, (iii) the Confidential Information of the Company to which the Restricted Party had, has or will have access, (iv) the fact that a business that competes with Alliqua Entities could greatly benefit if it were to obtain the Confidential Information of the Company, (v) the Alliqua Entities acquisition of the Company Intellectual Property and goodwill of the Company pursuant to the transactions contemplated by the Contribution and Merger Agreement, (vi) the fact that the Restricted Party would have an unfair competitive advantage if the Restricted Party were allowed to engage in the competitive activities prohibited by this Agreement in light of the Confidential Information and/or goodwill that the Restricted Party has acquired, and (vii) the provisions of this Agreement are reasonable and necessary to protect and preserve the Alliqua Entities’ interests in and right to use and operate the Company’s business from and after Closing.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the Restricted Party from disputing the Alliqua Entities' ownership rights of both the Confidential Information and the Company Intellectual Property. The changed text removes the prohibition against disputing the ownership of the Company Intellectual Property, while Section 3(c)(v) still refers to the Alliqua Entities' acquisition of the Company Intellectual Property. This creates a contradiction because Section 3(c)(v) implies ownership, but Section 4, as changed, no longer explicitly prevents the Restricted Party from disputing that ownership, leading to uncertainty about the Restricted Party's obligations and creating a potential legal issue, as defined by Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, the covenants in this Section 4 shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Restricted Party is required to disclose by applicable Law, after providing written notice to the Alliqua Entities before producing the information, which notice shall be given as soon as practicable, so that the Alliqua Entities, at their sole expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: The Restricted Party shall hold all Confidential Information in the strictest confidence.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing a conditional disclosure clause with a statement of strict confidence, this change creates a direct contradiction. It removes the allowed disclosure of confidential information when required by law, while other clauses still assume that such a lawful disclosure is possible, thus contradicting the Removed text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Restricted Party agrees not to dispute, contest, or deny any ownership rights of the Alliqua Entities of the Confidential Information. The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information. The Restricted Party shall not directly or indirectly, disclose to any unauthorized person or use for the Restricted Party’s own account any Confidential Information. The Restricted Party shall hold all Confidential Information in the strictest confidence.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for disclosure of confidential information if required by law, with a notification provision. The changed text mandates the 'strictest confidence,' eliminating this exception. The contradicted text also states that the Restricted Party shall hold all Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, which aligns with the Changed Text. However, because the changed text directly removes a previously allowed action (disclosure if required by law) creating a direct conflict with the assumptions that such lawful disclosure is possible elsewhere in the contract as mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation, it is considered a strong in-text contradiction. It alters a fundamental obligation related to confidential information, thus a 'YES' is justified.

perturbation.original_text: (d) The Restricted Party shall not, and shall cause any Affiliates not to, disparage or initiate negative publicity about the Company, the Alliqua Entities, or any of their respective employees, agents, business, policies, products, or services.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) The Restricted Party shall not, and shall cause any Affiliates not to, disparage or initiate negative publicity about the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the disparagement clause to only apply to the Company and not the Alliqua entities. It creates a contradiction because the agreement states 'the Restricted Party shall not, and shall cause any Affiliates not to, disparage or initiate negative publicity about the Company'. While other sections, specifically 3(a) mentions the importance of the Alliqua entities, thus creating a contradiction because the protection and good will does not extend to the Alliqua entities by name.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) This Agreement is entered into in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Contribution and Merger Agreement. The parties acknowledge that it would be detrimental to the Alliqua Entities if the Restricted Party were to compete with the Alliqua Entities any time during the Restricted Period or to solicit customers, clients, employees or contractors of the Company or Alliqua Entities at any time during the Restricted Period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the scope of the disparagement clause, removing 'the Alliqua Entities, or any of their respective employees, agents, business, policies, products, or services' from the original text, and limiting it to only 'the Company'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text (section 3(a)), which explicitly states the importance and potential detriment to the 'Alliqua Entities' if the Restricted Party were to compete or solicit. By removing the explicit protection of the 'Alliqua Entities' from disparagement, the changed text directly conflicts with the intent and obligations outlined in section 3(a), creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language protecting the Alliqua Entities has been removed, contradicting the obligations and constraints stated in another section.

perturbation.original_text: The Restricted Party agrees that in the event any of the covenants contained herein shall be held by any court to be effective in any particular area or jurisdiction only if said covenant is modified to limit its duration or scope, then the court shall have such authority to so reform the covenant and the parties hereto shall consider such covenant(s) and/or other provisions to be amended and modified with respect to that particular area or jurisdiction so as to comply with the order of any such court and, as to all other jurisdictions, the covenants contained herein shall remain in full force and effect as originally written.
perturbation.changed_text: The Restricted Party agrees that in the event any of the covenants contained herein shall be held by any court to be effective in any particular area or jurisdiction only if said covenant is modified to limit its duration or scope, then the court shall not have such authority to so reform the covenant.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the court's authority, where it was intended to be that the court would have authority to reform the covenant, to not having authority, creates an in text contradiction in section 6 Remedies. Section 6 expresses the rights to seek temporary restraining order and injunctive relief, which contradicts section 8 now, if the court doesn't have the authority to reform the covenant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Restricted Party acknowledges that the restrictions contained herein, in view of the nature of the Company’s business and the businesses of the Alliqua Entities, are reasonable and necessary to protect their legitimate business interests, business goodwill and reputation, and that any violation of these restrictions would result in irreparable injury and continuing damage to them, and that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek a temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining the Restricted Party from the commission of any breach or threatened breach of any covenants herein, without the necessity of establishing irreparable harm or the posting of a bond, and to recover from the Restricted Party damages incurred, as well as the attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses related to any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and enforcement of this Agreement. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as prohibiting the Alliqua Entities from pursuing any other remedies available to them for any breach or threatened breach, including, without limitation, the recovery of money damages, attorneys’ fees, and costs. The existence of any claim or cause of action by the Restricted Party, whether predicated on this Agreement or otherwise, shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement of the restrictive covenants contained herein, or preclude injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed a court to reform the restrictive covenant to make it enforceable. The changed text removes that ability. The contradicted text explicitly allows for injunctive relief and temporary restraining orders, which are remedies that rely on the enforceability of the covenant. If the court cannot reform the covenant, it might not be enforceable, thus contradicting the ability to seek injunctive relief as it's harder to obtain if the covenant is deemed unenforceable or overly broad.
